segments.text: There is a massive protest going around UGC's new bill. Hashtags like roll back UGC and black law are trending on social
media. Basically, under the new regulations, every college and university must set up an equal opportunity center, equity committee,
and equity squads. These bodies will monitor discrimination on campus and help victims access legal support if needed. This includes members from
scheduled cast, scheduled tribe, OBC communities, women, and persons with disabilities. These rules were introduced after the Supreme Court's
intervention following the suicides of Rohit Vimula and Pile Darwi allegedly due to castbased discrimination. But there's massive backlash around this.
One, critics say the definition of discrimination is very vague and can be misused. Two, general category students are neither represented nor recognized
as possible victims. Three, the biggest concern is the removal of punishment for false complaints which existed in the draft but was dropped in the final
version. Now the Supreme Court has put the stay on this. What do you think about it?
